coreConcept: The Water Cycle is like Earth's giant recycling system for water! It's how water moves all around our planet, from the sky to the ground and back again, making sure we always have water to drink and for plants to grow.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: The Sun
keyActorsAndComponents.description: The Sun is like the engine of the water cycle, giving all the energy needed to make water warm up and float into the sky.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Oceans, Lakes, Rivers
keyActorsAndComponents.role: Major sources of water for evaporation and collection points.
keyActorsAndComponents.description: These are the big swimming pools and bathtubs of our planet where most of the water lives and starts its journey into the sky.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Clouds
keyActorsAndComponents.role: Form from condensed water vapor and release precipitation.
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Clouds are like fluffy white factories in the sky where tiny water droplets or ice crystals gather before falling back to Earth.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Plants
keyActorsAndComponents.role: Release water vapor into the atmosphere through transpiration.
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Plants drink water from the ground and then 'breathe out' some of it as invisible water vapor into the air, just like we breathe out!

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Gravity
keyActorsAndComponents.role: Pulls water down as precipitation and makes it flow across land.
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Gravity is the invisible force that pulls everything down, making rain fall from clouds and rivers flow downhill back to the oceans.

storyOfProcess.name: Evaporation
storyOfProcess.description: The Sun warms up water in oceans, lakes, and rivers, turning it into an invisible gas called water vapor that floats up into the sky.

storyOfProcess.name: Condensation
storyOfProcess.description: High up in the sky, the water vapor gets cold and turns back into tiny liquid water droplets or ice crystals, which then clump together to form clouds.

storyOfProcess.name: Precipitation
storyOfProcess.description: When clouds get too full of water droplets, they release the water back to Earth as rain, snow, sleet, or hail.

storyOfProcess.name: Collection and Runoff
storyOfProcess.description: The water that falls back to Earth collects in oceans, lakes, and rivers, or soaks into the ground, ready to start the journey all over again!

corePrinciples.name: Water Never Disappears
corePrinciples.description: Water on Earth is always the same water, just moving around in different forms – it's never created or destroyed, just recycled!

corePrinciples.name: Sunlight is the Engine
corePrinciples.description: The Sun's warmth is super important because it gives the energy needed to lift water into the sky and keep the whole cycle going.

corePrinciples.name: Always Moving
corePrinciples.description: The water cycle is always happening, all the time, everywhere on Earth, making sure our planet always has fresh water.
